name: Tropism Response Process
description: Tropism Response Process in Plants. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A50[Environmental Stimulus] --> B50[Directional Cue]
    B50 --> C50[Phototropism]
    B50 --> D50[Gravitropism]
    B50 --> E50[Thigmotropism]
    C50 --> F50[Hormone Redistribution]
    D50 --> F50
    E50 --> F50
    F50 --> G50[Asymmetric Growth]
    G50 --> H50[Orientation Change]
    
    style A50 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B50 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C50 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style D50 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style E50 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style F50 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style G50 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style H50 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Tropism Response Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: Plant Tropisms: A Multiscale Integration of Environmental Cues, Hormones, and Genes
sources.authors: Su, S.H.; Liu, C.; Su, J.; Ma, L.; Huang, B.; Zhang, H.

sources.title: GRAVITROPISM: Mechanism and Signaling Pathway of Gravitropism in Plants
Phototropism in Higher Plants
sources.journal: Annu Rev Plant Physiol Plant Mol Biol

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
